identity.name: MANO
identity.description: A fraud protection platform that feels like a personal bodyguard, not a complex IT tool. It balances professional security with family-friendly accessibility.

headings.usage: Use for all major headings. Tracking tight (-0.02em). Weight 600 for standard, 400 for large display text.

body.usage: Use for all body text, UI labels, and data. Excellent legibility for seniors.

primary.name: Indigo Shield
primary.usage: Primary actions, brand identity, 'Protection Active' state.

destructive.name: Critical Rose
destructive.usage: Blocked threats, critical alerts, delete actions.

rules: No 'floating' elements; everything anchored to the grid.
Use 'Sidebar' navigation for desktop, 'Bottom Bar' for mobile.

cards.glass: Use 'Glassmorphism' only for sticky headers, not content cards.

visual_enhancers: Use 'Grain' texture overlays on solid backgrounds to reduce digital flatness.

micro_interactions: Lists stagger in (0.05s delay per item).

landing_hero.usage: Hero section background or main visual.

security_abstract.usage: Backgrounds for threat analysis sections (Use as fallback if CSS shield isn't used).

universal_guidelines: DO NOT use generic 'Cybersecurity Green' matrix effects.
DO NOT use 'Inter' font. Use 'Outfit' for headings.
Ensure all text has APCA compliant contrast (especially for Seniors).
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur) for headers/overlays.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠 for icons.
instructions_to_main_agent: Implement the 'Protective Swiss' aesthetic. Start with the Landing Page using the Bento Grid. Then build the Dashboard with the High Density grid. Ensure the 'Senior Mode' is a distinct, simplified view. Use the provided image URLs. For the 'Shield' visual, prefer a CSS/SVG implementation of a pulsing shield over a stock photo if possible.
